title: Review and setlist: Waxahatchee and MJ Lenderman perfectly complement one another at the Wang Theatre
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The acoustic, co-headlining set brought two of indie rock's biggest stars together in Boston.
MJ Lenderman and Waxahatchee perform live at the Wang Theatre in Boston on April 22, 2026.
Ross Cristantiello/Boston.

title: UMass Amherst chef charged with wife’s murder in campus hotel room
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Police say Jeffrey MacDonald admitted to beating his wife to death intentionally.
A student walks by Hotel UMass.

Jonathan Wiggs / The Boston Globe A man who’s reportedly an award-winning UMass Amherst chef has been arrested and charged with murder in connection with the death of his wife, who was also a UMass employee.

title: TD Garden unveils $100M revamp of premium suites
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Construction of the 83 suites will begin this summer, adding high-end furnishes and an upgraded culinary program.
An exterior view of TD Garden.

Adam Glanzman, Getty Images North America TD Garden plans to invest $100 million to rebuild all 83 of its premium suites, introducing high-end furnishings and an upgraded food program, the arena announced Thursday.

title: Violists serenade customers at Quincy Gas
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News Drivers got more than fuel, as musicians played personalized serenades at the pump.
From left to right: Luke Norman, Vincent Francis, Harvey Kertzman, Caroline Leguia, and Emily Tardiss at Quincy Gas on April 10, 2026.
(Photo courtesy of Harvey Kertzman) By Annie Jonas April 23, 2026 | 12:27 PM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Album review: Noah Kahan’s ‘The Great Divide’ showcases the artist’s staying power
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Music The Vermont-grown singer-songwriter teeters on the edge of an internal divide: who he was before his meteoric rise and where he goes from here.
Noah Kahan.
Patrick McCormack ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Tree House Brewing eyeing Faneuil Hall as next brewery location
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food News If approved, the site would include a brewery, distillery, and beer garden.
Tree House has applied to open a brewery at Faneuil Hall.
Courtesy of Tree House Brewing One of the most acclaimed independent breweries in the country has its sights set on the historic Faneuil Hall for another brewery and distillery location.

title: Massachusetts tops U.S. in AI job loss risk, Tufts report says
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Technology A new report estimates 7.
35% of jobs in Massachusetts are at risk of displacement from artificial intelligence, the highest share in the nation.
Aerials of Seaport District always in a growth mode of construction.

title: The Red Sox are reeling, but the Revs are rising
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morning Sports Update Don't look now, but New England's MLS team is on a hot streak.
Peyton Miller during the Revolution's 2-1 comeback win over Atlanta United.

via New England Revolution By Hayden Bird April 23, 2026 | 11:04 AM 4 minutes to read A tale of two season starts: On Wednesday, while most eyes in New England sports were fixated on the classic Red Sox-Yankees rivalry, another local team was engineering its second straight comeback win.

title: Video: Mass. family reels in great white shark off Florida coast
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News “In Fort Lauderdale, catching one is like getting a Super Bowl ring,” the boat’s captain says.
Captain's mate Logan Groff helps reel in the 10-foot great white shark.
Courtesy of Fishing Headquarters A Massachusetts family on vacation in South Florida reeled in an unexpected catch: a 10-foot great white shark.
